results.rendered_html:
upa-√kram P. -krāmati (rarely -kramati) Ā. -kramate, to go near, approach, come to,  RV. viii, 1, 4 ; 21, 2 ; 81, 7 ;  MBh. ;  R. ;  Megh. ;
to rush upon, attack (only P. by,  Kāś.  on  Pāṇ. i, 3, 39  and 42 ),  MBh. xiii ;
to approach with any object, have recourse to, set about, undertake, begin (with acc. dat. or inf. only Ā. by,  Pāṇ. i, 3, 39  & 42 ),  Lāṭy. ;  MBh. ;  Ragh.  &c.;
to treat, attend on (as a physician),  MBh. ;  Suśr.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
upakram 1 A., 4 P.
1 To go near, approach, come to, advance towards; rājñastasyājñayā devī vasiṣṭhamupacakrame  Mb. ;  Bk. 8. 25. 
2 To do, perform, set about; yathoktaṃ nipuṇamupakrāṃtavān  Dk. 120 ; mādhavasamakṣamuttaramupakramiṣye  Māl. 3 , 2. 7 ;  Y. 3. 200. 
3 To begin, commence. (with inf. or dat.); (Atm.); prasabhaṃ vaktumupakrameta kaḥ  Ki. 2. 28 ; pratigrahītuṃ… trilocanastāmupacakrame ca  Ku. 3. 66 ;  R. 17. 13. 
4 To go against, attack, assail, upacakramāte vāgbhiḥ  Rām. 
5 To make advances (of love) to; sarvopāyairupakramya sītāṃ  Rām. ; win over;  Dk. 65. 
6 To step over, stride, traverse.
7 To treat, act towards, attend on (as a physician); cure or heal
(pb) (as wounds); yatnādupakramyatāṃ  Ś. 3  v. l.; sarvathopakrāṃtaḥ  Dk. 68 ; ˚vraṇā 75.
